subject: Economics
overall_learning_objectives: Understand poverty as a multi-dimensional challenge
Identify how the poverty line is estimated in India
Recognize the social and economic groups vulnerable to poverty
Learn about inter-state disparities in poverty
Understand the major causes and anti-poverty measures taken by the government

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Poverty
topics_covered: Two typical cases of poverty: Urban and Rural
Dimensions of poverty: Hunger, lack of shelter, clean water, and sanitation
Poverty as seen by social scientists
teaching_strategy: Storytelling using the cases of Ram Saran and Lakha Singh
Brainstorming on the visible signs of poverty
student_activities: List 5 basic requirements a poor family struggles to meet
Compare the urban and rural case studies from the text
assessment: Oral questioning on basic poverty indicators

lesson_plan.title: Social Exclusion and Vulnerability
teaching_strategy: Discussion with real-life examples of social barriers
Explaining vulnerability as a 'probability' of remaining poor
student_activities: Identify groups in society that might face social exclusion
Write a short note on how natural disasters affect the vulnerable

lesson_plan.title: The Poverty Line
topics_covered: Estimation based on Calories (2400 rural/2100 urban)
Monetary value of the poverty line in India
teaching_strategy: Board demonstration of calorie vs. income requirements
Analysis of why rural calorie needs are higher
student_activities: Draw a chart showing the different calorie requirements
Identify the organization (NSSO) that estimates poverty
assessment: Quiz on calorie and income data

lesson_plan.title: Vulnerable Groups & Inequality
topics_covered: Economic groups: Agricultural and casual laborers
Inequality within the family: Women, elderly, and children
teaching_strategy: Using bar graphs to show poverty ratios among groups
student_activities: Analyze the graph of vulnerable groups from the NCERT book
Role-play a discussion on resource sharing within a poor household

lesson_plan.title: Inter-State Disparities
topics_covered: States that succeeded in reducing poverty (Kerala, Punjab, West Bengal)
teaching_strategy: Map-based activity to identify poverty levels
Discussion on state-specific success factors (e.g., Land reform, Education)
student_activities: Color a map of India to show high and low poverty states
List reasons why Kerala succeeded in poverty reduction
assessment: Short answer test on state disparities

lesson_plan.title: Causes and Anti-Poverty Measures
teaching_strategy: Detailed look at the 100-day work guarantee scheme
student_activities: Create a poster for the MGNREGA scheme
Write the full form and one feature of various government schemes

lesson_plan.title: Summary and Revision
topics_covered: Recap of poverty line, vulnerability, and inter-state gaps
teaching_strategy: Mind mapping of the entire chapter
student_activities: Participate in a chapter-end group quiz

teaching_aids: Charts showing Poverty Ratios by Group
Pie chart of rural vs. urban poverty distribution
teacher_tip: Focus on the 'Calorie Paradox' (why rural needs are higher but income limits are lower) to help students understand the logic behind the Indian poverty line estimation.
